data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57c40485">
    <title>execute-bead workers hold git index.lock for minutes; need lifetime tracking + fail-fast</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="fc88a9812c6535667cc858dc2fbc1270aa9f4481" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T112923-269b3a3d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

pattern.description: Regular expression to search for

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

operation.description: The operation to perform

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>

  </governing>

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>

ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="fc88a9812c6535667cc858dc2fbc1270aa9f4481" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T112923-269b3a3d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

pattern.description: Regular expression to search for

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

operation.description: The operation to perform

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57c40485">
    <title>execute-bead workers hold git index.lock for minutes; need lifetime tracking + fail-fast</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="fc88a9812c6535667cc858dc2fbc1270aa9f4481" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T112923-269b3a3d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

pattern.description: Regular expression to search for

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

operation.description: The operation to perform

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57c40485">
    <title>execute-bead workers hold git index.lock for minutes; need lifetime tracking + fail-fast</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="fc88a9812c6535667cc858dc2fbc1270aa9f4481" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T112923-269b3a3d"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57c40485">
    <title>execute-bead workers hold git index.lock for minutes; need lifetime tracking + fail-fast</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>

    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read file contents. Use instead of cat/head/tail.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

offset.description: 0-based line offset to start reading from

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to return

tools.name: write
tools.description: Create or overwrite a file with the given content. Creates parent directories as needed.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file using exact string replacement.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

oldText.description: Exact text to find (must be unique in the file and must not overlap with other edits)

old_string.description: Legacy: exact string to find (use edits[] instead)

tools.name: bash
tools.description: Execute a shell command for targeted repo operations such as builds, tests, or git. Do not use this for file discovery or file reading; use the find, grep, ls, and read tools instead. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code.

tools.name: find
tools.description: Find files by path pattern. Use this instead of shell find or ls when locating files by name.

pattern.description: Path pattern to match (e.g. '**/*.go', 'cmd/**/main.go'). Use ** to match across directories.

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents for a regex pattern. Use instead of grep/rg shell commands.

pattern.description: Regular expression to search for

dir.description: Directory to search in (relative or absolute; defaults to working directory)

glob.description: Restrict to files whose base names match this glob (e.g. '*.go', '*.ts')

exclude_dirs.description: Override default excluded directories. By default, skips .git, .hg, .svn, node_modules, and vendor/. Set to empty array [] to search all directories.

tools.name: ls
tools.description: List directory contents. Use instead of ls.

path.description: Directory to list (relative to working directory or absolute; defaults to working directory)

tools.name: patch
tools.description: Make targeted edits to a file. Supports: replace (default), replace_all, prepend, and append operations.

path.description: File path (relative to working directory or absolute)

search.description: Exact text to find in the file. Must match exactly (including whitespace). Will match first occurrence.

content.description: Replacement text, or text to prepend/append

operation.description: One of: 'replace' (default, search must be unique), 'replace_all' (replace every occurrence), 'prepend' (insert at file start), 'append' (insert at file end)

tools.name: task
tools.description: Track subtasks in a multi-step plan: create, update, get, list.

operation.description: The operation to perform

id.description: Task ID (required for update/get, ignored for create/list)

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>

5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>

<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-57c40485">
    <title>execute-bead workers hold git index.lock for minutes; need lifetime tracking + fail-fast</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch workers running execute-bead can hold the host repo's .git/index.lock for many minutes during a single bead execution. This serializes every other tracker-affecting operation in that repo (filing beads, dep edits, commits) and produces cascading tracker-lock-timeout failures for operators and other workers.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-17 21:38–21:44+ EDT)
- .git/index.lock mtime 21:38; still held at 21:44 = 6+ minutes uninterrupted.
- Holder: ddx work --watch (PID 90753) running execute-bead for fizeau-4b76e6b8 in worktree .ddx/executions/20260518T013652-2344d0e4.
- Side-effects observed in the operator session: bead create commands intermittently failed with 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/.git-tracker.lock, owner pid: 2615737)' and 'tracker lock timeout (max elapsed, lock: ..., owner pid: 2809592)'. Both the git index.lock and the .ddx/.git-tracker.lock exhibit the same pattern: held far longer than the operation needs.
- Five concurrent ddx work --watch daemons (PIDs 87523, 88624, 89333, 90753, 949868) plus a worker-spawned claude subprocess. The lock is held across the entire claude subprocess lifetime, not just the actual git mutation.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
git's index.lock and any short-lived mutation lock should be held only for the duration of the mutation itself (single-digit seconds at most). Holding it across the full bead-execution lifetime (which includes a multi-minute LLM subprocess) means:
1. Any concurrent ddx bead create/update/close from operators or other workers may time out.
2. Operators cannot do tracker hygiene (annotate findings, file follow-ons) while a long bead runs.
3. The error surface is misleading — 'tracker lock timeout' looks like a tracker bug, but the actual culprit is the index/staging-area lock.
4. Other ddx work --watch daemons sit idle when they could be making progress on independent beads.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Scope every git mutation lock to the minimum operation, never across the LLM subprocess invocation. Specifically: do not hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock while ddx is waiting on a claude/codex/gemini subprocess to return.
B. Instrument lock lifetime — emit a structured metric (lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release.
C. Fail-fast threshold — abort with a loud error if any worker holds a lock beyond a configured cap (proposed default: 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock). Hung workers should release and let others through, not block the whole tracker.
D. CI / load test — a multi-worker scenario test that proves 5 workers can each acquire and release the lock with p99 hold time under the cap.

CROSS-REF
Filed from Fizeau alignment-review session 2026-05-17 (AR-2026-05-17-repo). Originally surfaced during the no-static-fallback bead chain (parent fizeau-89eca738). No local Fizeau bead — this is a DDx infrastructure bug, filing directly here per AGENTS.md cross-repo triage pattern.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. ddx work --watch never holds .git/index.lock across an LLM subprocess (claude/codex/gemini/etc.) invocation; lock is released before the subprocess starts and re-acquired only for the actual git mutation. Verified by an integration test that runs an execute-bead and asserts .git/index.lock mtime is older than the subprocess invocation timeline.
2. Same property for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock: scope to the actual tracker file write, not across subprocess wait.
3. Lock lifetime metric is emitted as a structured event (JSON line with lock_name, acquired_at, released_at, duration_ms, holder_pid, operation) for every acquire/release; visible in ddx logs.
4. A configurable hold-time cap (default 10s for index.lock, 30s for tracker lock) is enforced: if a holder exceeds the cap, the worker logs an error, releases, and surfaces the violation in its execution evidence.
5. A multi-worker integration test (5+ concurrent ddx work --watch on independent beads) asserts p99 lock hold time stays under the cap and no operator-issued ddx bead create/update/close in a sibling shell times out.
6. Documentation update in ddx README or AGENTS.md naming the lock-lifetime contract.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, observability, priority:reliability, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>
